channel.title: Comments for page "A Business Dinner"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "A Business Dinner"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed the nitpicked things. Dunno how I missed those dashes, they had to be removed.

At some points, I also had some trouble understanding who said what, so try to keep each character's entire response in the same paragraph. Don't be afraid to include, he said. when one character's dialogue and a different character's actions are in the same paragraph.

I'll keep that in mind. Maybe will try to restructure some of the dialogue parts after the voting period is done.

Thanks a lot for your upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Easy upvote for me. I liked the meeting's hook, where it mentioned that two senators and a CEO were trapped inside a dream. I also liked the lead-in to the reveal that Aldrin is attending the meeting inside a dream.

+ Small nitpicks
– hide block

My only criticism is a small dialogue tag inconsistency throughout the article. For instance:

"Good. We are ready to provide such a service. Once," - Temnikov said with pressure, - as a gesture of good will and a demonstration of reliability. I guess we can sign the collaboration agreement right now."

The dialogue here needs to be enclosed in quotations, and I don't think dashes work to signify who spoke here; commas should do it.

"Yes, Mr. Aldrin, that's right. But I want to assure you right away that this dream is fully yours. Everything around," the man spread his arms, "Is your creation. […]"

"the man spread his arms" doesn't align with the surrounding dialogue, since it's a separate action. Simply replace each respective comma with a period, which would improve the flow.

At some points, I also had some trouble understanding who said what, so try to keep each character's entire response in the same paragraph. Don't be afraid to include , he said. when one character's dialogue and a different character's actions are in the same paragraph.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason I could literally hear and feel everything in this tale. +1 definetely. Good luck, comrade ;)))

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks a lot. I'm going to fix the listed errors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a very good read!
I did notice some errors (theres a broken link, the sentence starting with "hurricane " should probably have an "a"before it, it said "kerchief" itnstead of "handkerchief at one point) but either way this is a deserved +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was beautifully done. The rabbit changing to veal was an especially nice touch.

As an enthusiastic lucid dreamer, I had particular appreciation for the Collective's technique.

item.title: Co-author post
item.content:encoded:
A Collabcon entry written by myself and Arbelict.

Thanks to Gene R for fixing some grammar stuff, also to BlueJones and TheBlueHour for taking a look at it at the draft stage. Thanks to every one of you for reading, too.

The image can be found here and is public domain.
